items.subject: Product Update: Upgrade to Fortinet FortiGate (BYOL) Next-Generation Firewall new version
items.body_text:
[AWS Marketplace]
You don't often get email from no-reply@marketplace.aws. Learn why this is important 

Greetings from AWS Marketplace, 
Thank you for subscribing to Fortinet FortiGate (BYOL) Next-Generation Firewall. 
We are writing to inform you that, as of 2026-04-22, Fortinet Inc. will no longer offer versions listed below to new subscribers. Your use and subscription is unaffected for this version(s), however it is recommended that users upgrade to the latest version on AWS Marketplace. 
Versions no longer offered by seller: 
6.4.15
6.4.16
7.0.19

For additional questions or upgrade information, please contact Fortinet Inc. directly. Visit seller's profile page on AWS Marketplace. 
Thank you, 
The AWS Marketplace Team 
Visit AWS Marketplace. 
If you would no longer like to receive email notifications about this topic or would like to make other changes, you can manage your preferences.

items.body_text:
[AWS Marketplace]
You don't often get email from no-reply@marketplace.aws. Learn why this is important 

Greetings from AWS Marketplace, 
Thank you for subscribing to Fortinet FortiGate (BYOL) Next-Generation Firewall. 
We are writing to inform you that Fortinet Inc. has added a new version to Fortinet FortiGate (BYOL) Next-Generation Firewall on AWS Marketplace. As an existing customer, your subscription to the product, any running instances and access to previous versions are unaffected. However, Fortinet Inc. does recommend you to update to the latest version, Fortinet FortiGate (BYOL) Next-Generation Firewall/8.0.0 by visiting https://aws.amazon.com/marketplace/pp/prodview-lvfwuztjwe5b2. 
For additional questions or upgrade information, please contact Fortinet Inc. directly. Visit the seller's profile page on AWS Marketplace. 
Release notes for Fortinet FortiGate (BYOL) Next-Generation Firewall/8.0.0: 
https://docs.fortinet.com/document/fortigate/8.0.0/fortios-release-notes/ 
Thank you, 
The AWS Marketplace Team 
Visit AWS Marketplace.

items.subject: Closing the Gaps in Cross-Functional Execution
items.body_text:
[Spencer Johnson]
You don't often get email from spencer.johnson@moxo.com. Learn why this is important 

Adam​, 

Just tried your line. 

Operations turns GTM strategy into execution — aligning every team to move as one. 

But when execution is spread across Salesforce, Slack, emails, and spreadsheets, even the best strategy starts to break down: 
Tasks slip through the cracks — missed handoffs, delayed delivery, failed SLAs.
Documentation errors — incomplete forms, compliance risks, audit failures.
No ownership visibility — duplicated work, slower execution.
Bottlenecked approvals — manual back-and-forths stalling initiatives.
Fragmented communication — siloed tools causing misalignment, rework, and frustration.
Many leaders are exploring how AI can drive efficiency in these critical processes. 

Moxo’s AI-agentic workflows automate and validate repetitive tasks without losing the human touch, keeping your team accountable while moving faster and reducing risk. 

If it’d be valuable, I’d be happy to showcase how support your efforts. 

Look forward hearing your feedback, 

 Spencer Johnson
Business Development Representative
spencer.johnson@moxo.com
moxo.com   |   Book time 

On Mon, Apr 20, 2026 at 12:30 PM, Spencer Johnson <spencer.johnson@moxo.com> wrote: 

Adam, 

At the heart of every successful GTM strategy is Operations. Whether it's multiple teams involved like Finance, Sales, CS, Compliance, IT, etc., operations is critical to building scalable processes that accelerate growth. 

Moxo serves as the AI operating system for operations teams - purpose-built to accelerate cross-departmental coordination, task collaboration, requirements gathering, and communication across critical processes: 

Finance – Strategic planning, forecast alignment, commissions, budget tracking.
Sales – Comp plan management, sign-offs, and approvals.
Customer Success – Streamline onboarding, billing, and visibility into key milestones.
Cross-functional visibility & execution – connect disparate data sources and communication (structured, unstructured) to ensure all teams involved are aligned throughout the process.
Leveraging AI to reduce redundant work by quickly addressing incomplete and validate documents/forms, support and optimize actions.
Would it be worth a quick conversation to see how Moxo can synergize your GTM teams and remove execution friction? 

Thank you, 

 Spencer Johnson
Business Development Representative
spencer.johnson@moxo.com
moxo.com   |   Book time

items.subject: RE: [External] Re: Pax8 Follow Up
items.body_text:
[Matthew Carter]
Hi Stephen,
Again, thanks for your participation. Ultimately, we found their program to be more robust, with stronger technical resources, a shorter billing cycle, and recognition as a top-tier AWS partner.
 
We appreciate the effort from you and your team and hope to have the opportunity to work together in the future.
 

Matthew Carter

VP, Project Management & Global Supply Chain
+1 (416) 847-0852
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Stephen Slattery <sslattery@pax8.com> 
Sent: April 21, 2026 10:28 AM
To: Matthew Carter <Matthew.Carter@aptum.com>; Allan Weiss <aweiss@pax8.com>
Cc: Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Rob Harding <rharding@pax8.com>; Erik Tanguay <Erik.Tanguay@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: [External] Re: Pax8 Follow Up
 

Matthew,

 

Thank you for the follow up. 

 

If we can do anything for you in the future or if you just want some advice outside of your normal means, feel free to reach out anytime.

 

I'm also curious what one or two things does the alternate aggregator offer that Pax8 did not.  I appreciate any feedback to make my firm better.

 

Many thanks, sir.  

Stephen Slattery

Director AWS Sales Strategy

+1 210 660 2790

pax8.com

Connect on LinkedIn

    

From: Matthew Carter <Matthew.Carter@aptum.com>
Sent: Tuesday, April 21, 2026 9:18 AM
To: Stephen Slattery <sslattery@pax8.com>; Allan Weiss <aweiss@pax8.com>
Cc: Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Rob Harding <rharding@pax8.com>; Erik Tanguay <Erik.Tanguay@aptum.com>
Subject: RE: [External] Re: Pax8 Follow Up 

 

Hi Stephen and Allan,
Thank you for your participation in our AWS project. After careful consideration, we’ve selected an alternate aggregator.
 
We appreciate the time and professionalism your team brought to this effort and will keep you in mind for future opportunities.
 
Thanks, 
 

Matthew Carter

VP, Project Management & Global Supply Chain
+1 (416) 847-0852
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Stephen Slattery <sslattery@pax8.com> 
Sent: February 12, 2026 11:01 AM
To: Allan Weiss <aweiss@pax8.com>; Matthew Carter <Matthew.Carter@aptum.com>
Cc: Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Marc Pare <Marc.Pare@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: [External] Re: Pax8 Follow Up
 

Matthew and Team,

 

Wanted to let you know that I have a meeting Monday morning to discuss the billing process approval for Aptum.

 

Are there any updates from your side?

 

 

Stephen Slattery
​​​​

Director of Solutions Consulting

+1 210 660 2790

pax8.com

Connect on LinkedIn

From: Stephen Slattery <sslattery@pax8.com>
Sent: Friday, February 6, 2026 10:08 AM
To: Allan Weiss <aweiss@pax8.com>; Matthew Carter <Matthew.Carter@aptum.com>
Cc: Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Marc Pare <Marc.Pare@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: [External] Re: Pax8 Follow Up 

 

Matthew,

 
We'd like to give an update on Billing Transfer and Marketing partnership.
 
Billing Transfer:
 
The April 1st commitment would not be a general availability experience so there are some things to consider.
    We cannot test a customer because AWS does not allow for a APN partner to straddle two distributors.
    Aptum would have to agree to an AWS Distributor Seller Agreement with Pax8 (see Onboarding Guide).  
    Billing will not be granular in the beginning.  There'll be summaries of costs but not individual line-item billing attributes.  This is an AWS limitation with their new billing transfer platform.
    8% off eligible billing attributes will have to be manual on the Pax8 in the beginning due to an AWS issue we caught as part of the pilot program.  I'm working on getting internal approval for that next week.  
    8% would apply to April's usage billing in May.
    From a technical perspective, Pax8 is set up for your move to the new AWS billing transfer platform. 
 
Here's the customer move process overview:
·                     Aptum creates a Program Management Account (PMA) in their AWS Console
·                     Pax8 send a billing transfer request to PMA and Aptum accepts
·                     Aptum creates a PMA in each end customer AWS Console
·                     Aptum sends a billing transfer request to end customer(s) and they accept
·                     Create the relationship in the APN
·                     Pax8 has no need to contact the end customer(s).
 
We can set up a call to dive deeper into this when we are ready and to show  your team the Pax8 marketplace with the invoice UI.
 
I'm sure your Team will have questions, and the expectation is we might not have all the answers as we continue to integrate and build the billing transfer platform moving towards general availability. 

 

Marketing Update:

 
For our MDF, it pertains to MSPs who host their own customer or prospect events, or who exhibit at/attend industry events with the purpose of generating business and ROI.
 
We do have an approval process here that requires you to provide details prior to our approval granted, and reimbursement is delivered after the event has been completed.
 
As for the Premier Program, those are marketing assets (hospitality suite access, tickets, branding, etc) with local sports franchises, negotiated by our team on your behalf. The program does not operate on a set % of your spend. It will depend on the spend, the franchise you wish to work with, and more. To nail that down to something more tangible, I'd want you to have a discussion with our Program Director, Leigh Nordhoff. I am happy to set that up anytime.

 

Let me know if there's a path forward here and then I'll follow up on my final approvals.  I really want to partner with Aptum and if we can get through this together, it'll be a testament to our partnership.  Can we earn your business?  

 

Thank you. 

 

Stephen Slattery

​​​​

Director of Solutions Consulting

+1 210 660 2790

pax8.com

Connect on LinkedIn

From: Stephen Slattery <sslattery@pax8.com>
Sent: Thursday, February 5, 2026 4:25 PM
To: Allan Weiss <aweiss@pax8.com>; Matthew Carter <Matthew.Carter@aptum.com>
Cc: Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Marc Pare <Marc.Pare@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: [External] Re: Pax8 Follow Up

 

Matthew,

 

I'm drafting the email. We have a path forward. I want my team to review it internally first and I'll send what I have in writing to you tomorrow, Friday, Feb 6, 2026.  

Stephen Slattery

​​​​

Director of Solutions Consulting

+1 210 660 2790

pax8.com

Connect on LinkedIn

From: Allan Weiss <aweiss@pax8.com>
Sent: Thursday, February 5, 2026 2:23 PM
To: Matthew Carter <Matthew.Carter@aptum.com>; Stephen Slattery <sslattery@pax8.com>
Cc: Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Marc Pare <Marc.Pare@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: [External] Re: Pax8 Follow Up

 

Hi Matthew,

 

I can confirm that we can offer MDF funding in alternative geos.

 

Cheers,

 

Allan

 

Book a call with me!

Allan Weiss

​​​​

Manager of Sales

(416) 458‑3351

pax8.com

1275 Avenue des Canadiens‑de‑Montréal
​Montreal, QC H3B 0G4

Book a Meeting

From: Matthew Carter <Matthew.Carter@aptum.com>
Sent: February 5, 2026 3:02 PM
To: Stephen Slattery <sslattery@pax8.com>; Allan Weiss <aweiss@pax8.com>
Cc: Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Marc Pare <Marc.Pare@aptum.com>
Subject: RE: [External] Re: Pax8 Follow Up

 
Hi Allen,
Any MDF updates on funding by geography?
 
Hi Stephen,  
Our preference is to have a firm April 1 date in-writing, can you commit?
 
Thanks
 

Matthew Carter
VP, Project Management & Global Supply Chain
+1 (416) 847-0852
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Stephen Slattery <sslattery@pax8.com>
Sent: February 3, 2026 7:03 PM
To: Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Allan Weiss <aweiss@pax8.com>; Marc Pare <marc.pare@aptum.com>; Matthew Carter <Matthew.Carter@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: [External] Re: Pax8 Follow Up
 
Matthew and Adam,
 
Rob is at an offsite Tuesday and Wednesday of this week so I'll sync with him soon and get back with you on the process to move customers over, the possibility of running a test move before April 1 and ultimately agreeing to an April 1st  billing transfer onboarding.
 
Allen is working on gathering details on Pax8 Premier as far as geography and typical ROI for these events.  We have additional MDFs we allocate for strategic Canadian partners like Aptum which we can discuss as our partnership solidifies. 
 
We'll be in touch. 
 
 

Stephen Slattery
​​​​

Director of Solutions Consulting

+1 210 660 2790

pax8.com

Connect on LinkedIn

From: Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>

items.subject: Everything you need to think clearly about Professional Services
items.body_text:
[TSIA]
Practical insight to help you decide what’s next, available to you at no cost.
 

 View this email in your browser 

Hi Adam,
Professional Services leaders, like you, are being asked to solve harder problems than ever.
How should delivery models evolve as AI accelerates productivity?
Which services still protect margins?
Where should teams invest—and where should they stop?

Start getting answers 

You don’t need more opinions to answer those questions. You need research, structure, and the right questions.
That’s why TSIA makes a broad set of Professional Services content available at no cost, so leaders can work through their toughest challenges with confidence.

  

In the TSIA Portal, you can access: 
Professional Services research and State of the Industry insights. 
Proven frameworks for utilization, pricing, and margin protection. 
Expert perspectives on AI’s impact on delivery economics. 
Webinars and podcasts with TSIA research leaders.   

This content is designed to help you:
Ask better questions internally.
Pressure-test assumptions.
Apply proven approaches without starting from scratch.
Whether you’re preparing for delivery model changes, rethinking pricing, or planning your next evolution, these resources are built to support you.

Explore free Professional Services research 

            

This email was sent by: TSIA
17065 Camino San Bernardo, Suite 150 San Diego, CA, 92127, US
Update Profile | Manage Subscriptions | Unsubscribe

The #1 Services CPQ Platform for IT Service Providers

 

Your Next SOW Has Problems You Haven't Found Yet
 
Every SOW has the same issues. The margin gaps. The ambiguous deliverables. The scope that looks complete until delivery asks what "basic configuration" actually means.
 
The problem isn't that teams write bad SOWs. It's that the risks are invisible — until something goes wrong.
 
On May 6, ScopeStack founder Jon Scott is taking his red pen to a real Statement of Work — live. You'll watch as he identifies where margin is being left on the table, where risk is buried in the language, and how enterprise-quality SOWs are actually built.
 What You'll See in the Live Review
Where ambiguity hides – The phrases that sound complete but aren't — and what to replace them with.

The exclusion gaps – What's missing from the SOW that delivery will eventually argue about.

Margin risk language – How vague deliverables become scope creep after the contract is signed.

Enterprise vs. average SOW structure – What separates a bulletproof scope from a liability.

Real-time fixes – Corrections you can apply to your own scopes immediately.
 
This isn't a product demo. It's a hands-on look at the craft of scoping — from someone who's reviewed thousands of them.
 
May 6, 2026 | 1:00 PM EST | Live stream

Register for the Live SOW Teardown 

ScopeStack is trusted by leading solution providers.
Including teams at Computacenter, SHI, ePlus, NRI, Driven Tech, and more.
 
★★★★★ 4.9/5 on G2

 

“ScopeStack provides a thoughtful and flexible CPQ framework that allows us to enhance our Professional Service development and increase the quality of service and relevance to our internal and external Customers.”
 
— Simon D, Director Services Transformation - Computacenter
 

 

ScopeStack, PO BOX 26974, Greenville, SC 29616 
Unsubscribe Manage preferences

On April 21, TDSynnex updated root access, enabled MFA, and signed out—triggering a root activity alert for Basis.
Transition from Ingram led to deletion of a CloudCheckr stack (i.e., Security Breach); which is now replaced by TD’s StreamOne Ion portal.
Root access and transparency are critical to Basis; no unexpected changes going forward (see RCA).
Basis is unlikely to require full admin; will be offered 2–3 limited-access IAM options (e.g., billing, read-only).
Full root access can be restored if requested.
TDSynnex will only access root upon explicit request (and on initial setup).
No decisions made; meeting was informational only.
Customer deadline to select IAM access level: April 24.
Subsequent training led by Monica Boatman (primarily asynchronous) Monica.Boatman@tdsynnex.com
Erik Tangay to onboard Priscilla with portal/billing access.
Commercial terms: 8% discount confirmed on Basis products (excluding marketplace).
Invoices issued between 10th–15th of the following month; net 30 terms.
 

Matthew Carter
VP, Project Management & Global Supply Chain

+removing TDSynnex Perkins, Ciara ciara.perkins@tdsynnex.com & Kondek, Jan Jan.Kondek@tdsynnex.com
 
On April 21, TDSynnex updated root access, enabled MFA, and signed out—triggering a root activity alert for Basis. 
Transition from Ingram led to deletion of a CloudCheckr stack (i.e., Security Breach); which is now replaced by TD’s StreamOne Ion portal. 
Root access and transparency are critical to Basis; no unexpected changes going forward (see RCA). 
Basis is unlikely to require full admin; will be offered 2–3 limited-access IAM options (e.g., billing, read-only). 
Full root access can be restored if requested. 
TDSynnex will only access root upon explicit request (and on initial setup). 
No decisions made; meeting was informational only. 
Customer deadline to select IAM access level: April 24. 
Subsequent training led by Monica Boatman (primarily asynchronous) Monica.Boatman@tdsynnex.com
Erik Tangay to onboard Priscilla with portal/billing access. 
Commercial terms: 8% discount confirmed on Basis products (excluding marketplace). 
Invoices issued between 10th–15th of the following month; net 30 terms.
 

Matthew Carter

items.subject: FW: DRAFT - DO NOT DISTRIBUTE - Forecast
items.body_text:
[Marc Alex Forget]
Marc Alex Forget

Senior Vice-President of Sales and Marketing | Vice‑président principal, Ventes et Marketing
437-500-2700
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Tom Kelsch <Tom.Kelsch@aptum.com> 
Sent: Tuesday, April 14, 2026 9:43 PM
To: Marc Alex Forget <Marc-Alexandre.Forget@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: DRAFT - DO NOT DISTRIBUTE - Forecast
Sensitivity: Confidential
 

Hi MA,

 

Attached is an updated version of the analysis we reviewed earlier today.

 

I created a new view comparing our forecast accuracy over the last 12 months under the Current Methodology to the Proposed Methodology, as detailed in your email below.

 

All Opps: % of Forecast Achieved increased from 50% to 70%

New Logo: % of Forecast Achieved increased from 15% to 36%

Existing Customers: % of Forecast Achieved was unchanged at 84%

The reductions in forecast value from the stage changes were largely offset by the removal of the Forecast Category weighting for Existing Customer opps

 

The proposed methodology would have the following impact on the forecast for the rest of FY26:

 

 

There is also a table with the top 10 opps changes: we would see significant forecast reductions for WebNX, Croesus, Promutuel, Latitude, and others. 

 

Take a look and let me know if you have any questions.

 

Thanks,

 

Tom Kelsch

Senior Decision Support Analyst
aptum.com | LinkedIn

 

 

From: Marc Alex Forget <Marc-Alexandre.Forget@aptum.com>
Sent: Tuesday, April 14, 2026 2:47 PM
To: Tom Kelsch <Tom.Kelsch@aptum.com>
Cc: Marc Alex Forget <Marc-Alexandre.Forget@aptum.com>
Subject: DRAFT - DO NOT DISTRIBUTE - Forecast 

 
Tom,

As discussed, here are my working notes:
 
Sales Opportunity Management & Forecasting Framework
Objective
This framework is designed to improve forecast accuracy, simplify pipeline management, and elevate CRM discipline by:
·       Standardizing opportunity stages and weightings
·       Reducing unnecessary opportunity volume in Salesforce
·       Establishing clear governance and accountability for opportunity management
 
Opportunity Stage Consolidation & Forecast Methodology
To improve pipeline clarity and forecast reliability, opportunity stages will be reduced, clearly defined, and consistently applied.
 
Forecasting Approach
·       Next 60 days: Forecasts will be driven by standardized opportunity stage weightings.
 
Opportunity Stages & Definitions
Stage 1: Qualification — Weighted 0%
·       Customer problem and use case clearly confirmed (Story)
·       BANT validated and documented (Budget status, Authority, Need-Story, Timeline)
·       Customer buying timeline articulated and evidenced via CRM notes or email
Stage 2: Solution Development — New Logo Weighted 25% / Existing Weighted 60%
·       Solution scope and pricing defined and presented
·       BANT reconfirmed
·       Commercial discussions underway with the objective of securing verbal alignment on solution and pricing
Stage 3: Contract — New Logo Weighted 50% / Existing Weighted 80%
·       Order Form and/or Statement of Work issued and under customer review
·       Final approvals and signature pending
Stage 4: Closed (Won) — Weighted 100%
·       Contract fully executed
 
Ongoing Calibration
·       Win rates will be monitored by Product Category
·       Stage weightings will be adjusted over time to reflect actual conversion performance
·       This data will inform both forecast accuracy and individual sales performance measurement
 
Reduction of Salesforce Opportunity Volume
To reduce CRM clutter, improve data quality, and focus sales effort on revenue‑generating activity, opportunity ownership and creation will be streamlined as follows:
 
Sales Rep Ownership
·       Sales Reps will create opportunities only for:
o   New business
o   Named Account Migration opportunities
 
SDM & Operational Ownership
·       Add‑On removals and downgrades will be managed by the SDM team via Ocean
·       Ocean will automatically push all relevant changes into Salesforce
 
Forecasting & Automation
·       Forecasting for Add‑On removals will be based on historical monthly averages, ensuring predictability without increasing rep administrative burden
·       Only Renewals for accounts with a Named Account Manager will be tracked in Salesforce
·       Renewal automation for all other accounts will be delivered in partnership with the SDM team
 
Opportunity Governance & CRM Discipline
Strong governance is required to ensure data integrity, coaching effectiveness, and forecast confidence.
·       Sales Managers will conduct a weekly, structured review of opportunity management and CRM hygiene
·       All customer meetings, commercial discussions, and decision‑related interactions must be logged as Salesforce Activities to maintain a complete and auditable history
·       Opportunities must remain in Stage 1 (Qualification) until the customer’s buying timeline is clearly articulated and documented
·       These reviews will be used to:
o   Reinforce accountability
o   Enable consistent coaching
o   Identify forecast risk early and proactively
 
Forecast Category
For Manager overwrite only Omitted / Commit.
 
Success Measures
Success will be tracked through:
·       Improved forecast accuracy and predictability
·       Reduced low value opportunity volume per sales rep
·       Higher stage‑to‑stage conversion confidence
·       Improved win‑rate visibility by Product Category

 
Marc Alex Forget
Senior Vice-President of Sales and Marketing | Vice‑président principal, Ventes et Marketing
437-500-2700
aptum.com | LinkedIn

Subject: RE: AWS Cloud Operations Competency App-0059154 - Aptum
 

You don't often get email from pacolor@amazon.es. Learn why this is important
Hello team,
 
Thank you again for your interest in the AWS Competency Program. Our Segment team has completed their review and have identified a few areas where providing additional information or clarification could strengthen your case studies and ensure they better align with the program's requirements. Please take a moment to review the recommendations below.
 
Action Items:
Please address the following items for remediation flagged by our Segment team to continue processing your application.  To ensure your application is processed, please provide updates by date – within 10 business days. Applications for AWS Cloud Operations Competency will remain open for 10 business days at which point your application will be closed until we hear back from you.
 
Thank you for submitting your self-assessment for the AWS Cloud Operations Competency — Monitoring & Observability category. I have completed our Business Review and are pleased to share that Aptum demonstrates strong technical alignment with the competency requirements. Your long-standing investment in Datadog, Elastic SIEM, and a mature managed cloud practice provides a compelling foundation.
 
That said, we have identified few documentation gaps that must be addressed before your application can advance to Technical Validation. Please review the feedback below and take the recommended actions prior to resubmission.
 
Overall Recommendation: Apply — After Gap Remediation
 
Estimated Time to Readiness: 2–4 weeks Confidence Level: High — gaps are documentation-related, not capability-related
Section 1: Required Documentation Uplifts
� Dashboard Access Control Policies (COAMO-002)
 
What is missing: Your self-assessment does not include documentation of how customer dashboard access is controlled within Datadog. The checklist requires explicit evidence of role-based access control (RBAC) policies governing who can view, edit, and manage observability dashboards.
 
What is required:
 
    1. A written description of your Datadog RBAC model (roles, permissions, and access tiers)
    2. At least one customer-specific example showing how access was configured (e.g., Futurion or Pool Supplies Canada)
    3. Supporting evidence such as a screenshot, configuration export, or Confluence runbook excerpt
 
Why this matters: This is a mandatory checklist item. Without it, the application will be flagged as incomplete during Technical Validation.
� Reference Architecture Diagrams (COAMO-001, COAMO-003, COAMO-005)
 
What is missing: Three reference architecture diagrams are required as formal evidence submissions and have not been confirmed as audit-ready:
 
    1. Monitoring & Observability Baseline (COAMO-001) — end-to-end telemetry architecture
    2. Container Observability (COAMO-003) — ECS/Fargate + Datadog APM architecture
    3. Synthetic Monitoring (COAMO-005) — Datadog synthetic test architecture with CloudWatch integration
 
What is required:
 
    1. Each diagram must show AWS services used, third-party tooling, data flows, and integration points
    2. Diagrams must be in a shareable format (PDF, PNG, or draw.io) and uploaded to your Partner Central application folder
    3. Diagrams should reference the specific customer deployment they represent (e.g., Pool Supplies Canada for container observability)
 
Why this matters: Architecture diagrams are a mandatory deliverable for each selected use case. Reviewers will use these to validate technical depth and AWS service integration.
 
� Quantifiable KPIs with Baseline Metrics (EXBL-003)
 
What is missing: Several case studies reference KPIs and outcomes but do not include before/after baseline comparisons. The checklist requires measurable improvements with quantified starting points.
 
What is required: For each of the 4 case studies, provide at least 2 KPIs in the following format:
 
   1. Metric name (e.g., Mean Time to Detect, Alert Noise Ratio, Uptime %)
    2. Baseline value (before Aptum engagement)
    3. Achieved value (after implementation)
    4. Timeframe for the improvement
 
Section 2: Items Correctly Addressed — No Action Required
 
The following areas were reviewed and found to be fully compliant:
 
    ✅ COAMO-001 Baseline Telemetry — Comprehensive coverage of infrastructure, service, and application layers
    ✅ COAMO-003 Container Observability — Two production examples (Pool Supplies ECS Fargate, Vimana Kubernetes) exceed the minimum requirement
    ✅ COAMO-005 Synthetic Monitoring — Clear evidence across Futurion and Heilind Electronics
    ✅ COAMO-006 Hybrid Cloud — Correctly marked N/A; no action required
    ✅ COAMO-007 GenAI Monitoring — Correctly excluded; aspirational responses noted but not penalized
    ✅ COBL-001 through COBL-004 — Baseline requirements met via Terraform + GitLab CI/CD across all case studies
 
Status: Conditonally Approved BR - Please update the response with the above recommendation before Technical Validation. Thank You.
 
If you have not yet explored the AWS Partner Specialization Program Benefits Guide, please make sure to see what will be waiting for you when you achieve your designation.
 
Thank you,
 
 
 

Francisco Lorenzo Fernández-Shaw
Tech Business Developer – PPS Team
Amazon Web Services – Spain | Madrid
pacolor@amazon.es

As the previous communication was conducted through my colleague's personal email, I unfortunately do not have access to the prior correspondence. Could you please forward the email thread to me? Lumen intends to carry out internal maintenance within its network. The nature of this work is to perform a fiber maintenance activity which is required in order to proactively avoid unplanned outages due to the splice case is stuck in cement.

items.subject: Agentic AI is here. Is your business ready?
items.body_text:
[Expedient]
Join us 5/19 for a practical conversation on moving AI from pilot to production.️ 

Can't see this email? View it in your browser.

 

Most organizations have experimented with AI, but few have operationalized it.

The gap is not technical capability. It is the ability to connect AI to real business processes, govern it properly, and prove ROI fast enough to justify continued investment.  

Join Brad Reynolds, SVP of AI at Expedient, and Thomas Cooper, Director of AI Product, on Tuesday, May 19 at 12:00 PM ET for a practical discussion on what separates AI experimentation from AI execution.  

You'll learn:
The difference between GenAI and Agentic AI, and where each fits in your stack
How to avoid pilot purgatory by starting with business processes, not technology features
Real workflows delivering measurable ROI: support ticket automation, QBR generation, RFP answering, and more 
All attendees will receive information on the Business Outcome Accelerator, a no-cost implementation of one agentic workflow to prove ROI before you buy.  
Save your seat   →
Can't attend live? Register anyway to receive the full replay and key takeaways. 

1 Allegheny Square
Pittsburgh, PA 15212
Learn more about Expedient
 

You are receiving this email because you previously opted in to receive communications from Expedient or its partners.
Click the link below to manage your preferences at any time.
update subscription preferences

items.subject: Adam , how much SLED revenue went unclaimed last year — and what to do about it
items.body_text:
[Peter Famiglietti]
You don't often get email from famiglietti@innovateaottechnologies.com. Learn why this is important 

Hi ,

I'm following up from my previous email. I know it's easy for these things to slip through when you're heads-down on active programs, so I'll keep this one focused and worth your time.

I want to ask you a straightforward question: how many SLED contracts has Aptum walked away from in the last 12 months — not because you didn't want them, but because you didn't have the technical depth to deliver them confidently?

It's a question most firms in this space don't ask out loud, but the answer matters more than most leaders realize. Every contract you couldn't respond to was revenue that went to someone else.

Here's what a SLED360 alliance partnership changes for firms like Aptum :
→  You respond to contracts you couldn't staff before
→  You deliver technical depth — cloud engineering, workflow automation, and AI — without hiring for it
→  Your clients see one team, one delivery standard, under your brand
→  You build a recurring capability that compounds with every engagement

This isn't a subcontracting arrangement. It's a structured way to grow what Aptum  can credibly offer — without the overhead of building it from scratch.

We're currently onboarding a small number of alliance partners this quarter. I'd love for Aptum  to be one of them.

Would a this week work for you?

Best regards,
Peter Famiglietti, MBA, PEng
Principal - Alliances, Partnerships, Co-Marketing 
Canada | USA | India
LinkedIn

Thanks for the update Matt.. Regrouping and reframing some of this.

TD Synnex said they will provide us with information about what access they have moving forward and what the "root" situation was yesterday. Everything below needs to be hashed out w/ TDS on a call... today before 3pm as Rob and David are meeting Andy and team at 6pm. 

Coordinate a call with Basis, Aptum, and TDSynnex (Erik & Barb) <-- @Matthew Carter, please coordinate this. Barb and Erik can support - Let's get the internal/TDS sync FIRST... then when we can decide if we need a call AFTER Rob/David meet w/ Andy/team today. 

Confirm restoration of root access or determine if a root exception addendum is required (Erik) <-- Needs to happen during the TDS call.

Provide additional clarity on the security breach component—was this a system-generated alert triggered by the root access change, or does it indicate something broader, such as environment exposure or credential compromise? (Marc-Alex/David/Rob)

Everything Else

Review Marc Pare’s Slack and/or email to confirm any prior commitments made to Basis (Barb/Sarah)
Confirm whether a new RCA is required and, if so, identify the owner of the previous report (Marc-Alex) <-- Barb is on this as of yesterday afternoon

Ensure Billing is aligned on the appropriate discount to apply to Basis’ April invoice (Priscillia) <-- I've already reached out to Rick on this one 

Here are the alerts that Basis saw (Brian's words):
Our Datadog SEIM alert

Four logins from 2 different IPs

The 165 address appears to be part of a Zscaler block, so I am guessing this was Aptum.  The 137 address is owned by Azure/Microsoft so I am assuming this was TD Synnex

I'm still digging into this, but it also appears from the SEIM signals that not only was there several root logins, the root user modified IAM for IMBillingRole (edited) 

The source was CloudFormation and the stack referenced in the logs does not exist, so if I had to guess it was someone deleting a stack that was necessary for Ingram but not for TD Synnex.  I'm still digging but it would be helpful if someone involved could just confirm "Yeah, we deleted a stack"

Hi Adam & Team,
 
I wanted to share the clarification via email as well to ensure the whole team was notified.
 

When a Partner moves from Disti A to Disti B, their discounts move with them. If a Program Account or Technology Engagement Program Account was Base eligible, it will continue to receive Base discount, regardless of spend, UNLESS EUR is changed to Internal. The same applies for any account receiving POD/PSD/CEI through the sunset period (up to March 31, 2027).

Meaning no Shareshift discount will apply here, and Aptum will receive the 8% front-end discount on all accounts/eligible AWS services regardless of spend. I've also attached a doc that outlines this as there are some exceptions, but it relates directly to Billing Transfer and NCI.

 
Regarding the customer receiving notifications of the root access transfer, I've provided a link here that outlines how TD SYNNEX manages access to these accounts after the CTA process is complete TD SYNNEX Commercial AWS Management Account Access Guide – DLT Operations Center. I'm adding in @Kondek, Jan here as he has offered to have a call with you and/or the end-customer to discuss our access to go over any questions/concerns. Additionally, we still need to deploy our access roles on the accounts, and there is a chance Centro (Basis) will receive notification of this event. If you would like to talk to the customer before we implement this please let us know and confirm when we can proceed as we have a deadline to complete this step.
 
 
Please let us know if you have any immediate questions or concerns and thank you all again for your continued support.
 
Best,

HI Marc,
 
Please find below the answers to your questions.
 
We have clients that are using AWS native tools for cost reporting and we are keen to understand when/what changes with their experience.
 
From your customer’s perspective, there should be no change to their experience when it comes to using AWS native cost reporting tools. Services such as Cost Explorer, CUR (Cost and Usage Reports), and other AWS-native billing and reporting capabilities will continue to function exactly as they do today.The transition to TD SYNNEX as the distribution partner primarily affects the billing and distribution relationship on the backend, not the operational environment within AWS.  
 
We are wondering if there is a two hop route to implementing this change and what the impact would be to our margin. Phase  1) just make TD our distribution , Phase 2) move some customers over to new model that removes Root for Distribution. 
 
Now, there isn’t a simple or automatic way to transfer accounts between different distribution models. While initiating the transition to TD SYNNEX as the distributor is certainly possible, moving accounts across structural models typically requires careful coordination and review on a case-by-case basis to ensure the transition is handled smoothly.
I would also recommend reviewing the exit clause with your current distributor. In most cases, partners are required to provide a 90-day notice period before making a move to another distributor. Ensuring this is aligned early in the process helps avoid any delays or contractual complications during the transition.
 
Kindly revert on any clarifications.
 
Thanks & Regards

Hi Christine, Gina & Rupesh
Please treat as priority and address comments in blue:
 
Custom cost-management tools and Security dashboards via StreamOne Ion?
Yes, the StreamOne platform provides pre-built dashboards and the ability to create custom reports, including configurable alerts. These reports can show usage, consumption trends, and other financial insights.
StreamOne also offers real-time reporting on IaaS consumption (Azure, AWS, and Google), enabling advanced cost-optimization capabilities and continuous billing visibility, not just month-end reporting.
In addition, StreamOne includes a SecOps Portal for partners, providing a centralized dashboard to view and manage customer security features and policies.
If you need more specific details, I’m happy to follow up.
Please provide detailed steps how you will assist us with the change-over from another aggregator to TDS?
Access to AWS funding programs, ACE deal registrations and leads?
Yes, As a Premier Tier AWS Partner, we help our partners leverage AWS mechanisms and programs, including offerings, funding, enablement, and guidance on ACE deal registrations as part of co-sell execution (where eligible and aligned to AWS criteria).
The TD SYNNEX AWS team also has a dedicated Customer Success and Conversion team focused on customer lead generation and demand generation. Who exactly would be part of our team? Where does Gina fit into the picture and how can we utilize her as a resource?
We’ve built an infrastructure framework around programs like SBAI and other third-party lead-gen mechanisms to maintain, hydrate, nurture, and route end-customer leads to our partners—across both Commercial and Public Sector.
API access or automated insights for billing data independent of AWS (i.e., aligning payment terms, managing outstanding balances are important to us)
Yes, both are available. I can connect you with the team that managed this process so they can provide a detailed overview and answer any specific questions.
What GTM strategies do you offer? Free training and discounted workshops? Co-sponsored AWS events?
Yes, TD SYNNEX offers several GTM motions to help partners build and scale pipeline, including enablement, co-marketing, and co-sell support.
Pipeline: Top-of-funnel, assessment-led sales plays for customers both on AWS and not yet on AWS.
Training: Fully subsidized training and enablement programs are available.
Events: Depending on the use case, eligibility, and cost, we can either fully subsidize or co-invest in joint webinars, SKOs, and customer-facing events—provided there’s a defined solution focus, target audience, pipeline target, and measurable ROI.
On average, how much MDF credits can we anticipate receiving monthly/annually from the TDSynnex program? We would like to see details as this is important to us.
Will we be assigned a dedicated account manager, technical expert or help with pre-sales and design? (saving strategies and rightsizing recommendations are critical).
Yes, you’ll have a dedicated AWS account manager, plus access to technical SMEs and pre-sales engineering resources. Again, who would be part of our team, Gina?

Hello Aptum team,

Nice to e-meet you all.

Thank you for your due diligence, you’re asking all the right questions. I did my best to respond succinctly in email, but I’d love to walk through each point in more depth on a call, especially if you’re interested in how we differentiate compared to other AWS distributors.
Custom cost-management tools and Security dashboards via StreamOne Ion?

Yes, the StreamOne platform provides pre-built dashboards and the ability to create custom reports, including configurable alerts. These reports can show usage, consumption trends, and other financial insights.

StreamOne also offers real-time reporting on IaaS consumption (Azure, AWS, and Google), enabling advanced cost-optimization capabilities and continuous billing visibility, not just month-end reporting.

In addition, StreamOne includes a SecOps Portal for partners, providing a centralized dashboard to view and manage customer security features and policies.

If you need more specific details, I’m happy to follow up.
Access to AWS funding programs, ACE deal registrations and leads?

Yes, As a Premier Tier AWS Partner, we help our partners leverage AWS mechanisms and programs, including offerings, funding, enablement, and guidance on ACE deal registrations as part of co-sell execution (where eligible and aligned to AWS criteria).

The TD SYNNEX AWS team also has a dedicated Customer Success and Conversion team focused on customer lead generation and demand generation.

We’ve built an infrastructure framework around programs like SBAI and other third-party lead-gen mechanisms to maintain, hydrate, nurture, and route end-customer leads to our partners—across both Commercial and Public Sector.
API access or automated insights for billing data independent of AWS (i.e., aligning payment terms, managing outstanding balances are important to us)
Yes, both are available. I can connect you with the team that managed this process so they can provide a detailed overview and answer any specific questions.
What GTM strategies do you offer? Free training and discounted workshops? Co-sponsored AWS events?

Yes, TD SYNNEX offers several GTM motions to help partners build and scale pipeline, including enablement, co-marketing, and co-sell support.

Pipeline: Top-of-funnel, assessment-led sales plays for customers both on AWS and not yet on AWS.
Training: Fully subsidized training and enablement programs are available.
Events: Depending on the use case, eligibility, and cost, we can either fully subsidize or co-invest in joint webinars, SKOs, and customer-facing events—provided there’s a defined solution focus, target audience, pipeline target, and measurable ROI.
Will we be assigned a dedicated account manager, technical expert or help with pre-sales and design? (saving strategies and rightsizing recommendations are critical).

Yes, you’ll have a dedicated AWS account manager, plus access to technical SMEs and pre-sales engineering resources.

Given the breadth of AWS service delivery designations and competencies (e.g., MSP, migrations, government, education, to name a few), our AWS practice provides technical expertise and support across migrations & modernization, managed services, professional services, assessments, and AWS workloads.

Additionally, our ServiceSolv team provides engineering, integration, deployment, and remediation services which enables you to offer end-to-end customer lifecycle support without building these functions internally. We can also provide white-labeled resources delivered under your branding, where applicable.
Net 90 terms
Extended payment terms are feasible through TD SYNNEX. We'd have to get you in contact with our credit/finance team to review your eligibility

Hi Adam & Team,
 
I wanted to share the clarification via email as well to ensure the whole team was notified.
 

When a Partner moves from Disti A to Disti B, their discounts move with them. If a Program Account or Technology Engagement Program Account was Base eligible, it will continue to receive Base discount, regardless of spend, UNLESS EUR is changed to Internal. The same applies for any account receiving POD/PSD/CEI through the sunset period (up to March 31, 2027).

Meaning no Shareshift discount will apply here, and Aptum will receive the 8% front-end discount on all accounts/eligible AWS services regardless of spend. I've also attached a doc that outlines this as there are some exceptions, but it relates directly to Billing Transfer and NCI.

 
Regarding the customer receiving notifications of the root access transfer, I've provided a link here that outlines how TD SYNNEX manages access to these accounts after the CTA process is complete TD SYNNEX Commercial AWS Management Account Access Guide – DLT Operations Center. I'm adding in @Kondek, Jan here as he has offered to have a call with you and/or the end-customer to discuss our access to go over any questions/concerns. If you would like to talk to the customer before we implement this please let us know and confirm when we can proceed as we have a deadline to complete this step.
 
 
Please let us know if you have any immediate questions or concerns and thank you all again for your continued support.

Hi Christine, Gina & Rupesh
Please treat as priority and address comments in blue:
 
Custom cost-management tools and Security dashboards via StreamOne Ion?
Yes, the StreamOne platform provides pre-built dashboards and the ability to create custom reports, including configurable alerts. These reports can show usage, consumption trends, and other financial insights.
StreamOne also offers real-time reporting on IaaS consumption (Azure, AWS, and Google), enabling advanced cost-optimization capabilities and continuous billing visibility, not just month-end reporting.
In addition, StreamOne includes a SecOps Portal for partners, providing a centralized dashboard to view and manage customer security features and policies.
If you need more specific details, I’m happy to follow up.
Please provide detailed steps how you will assist us with the change-over from another aggregator to TDS?
Access to AWS funding programs, ACE deal registrations and leads?
Yes, As a Premier Tier AWS Partner, we help our partners leverage AWS mechanisms and programs, including offerings, funding, enablement, and guidance on ACE deal registrations as part of co-sell execution (where eligible and aligned to AWS criteria).
The TD SYNNEX AWS team also has a dedicated Customer Success and Conversion team focused on customer lead generation and demand generation. Who exactly would be part of our team? Where does Gina fit into the picture and how can we utilize her as a resource?
We’ve built an infrastructure framework around programs like SBAI and other third-party lead-gen mechanisms to maintain, hydrate, nurture, and route end-customer leads to our partners—across both Commercial and Public Sector.
API access or automated insights for billing data independent of AWS (i.e., aligning payment terms, managing outstanding balances are important to us)
Yes, both are available. I can connect you with the team that managed this process so they can provide a detailed overview and answer any specific questions.
What GTM strategies do you offer? Free training and discounted workshops? Co-sponsored AWS events?
Yes, TD SYNNEX offers several GTM motions to help partners build and scale pipeline, including enablement, co-marketing, and co-sell support.
Pipeline: Top-of-funnel, assessment-led sales plays for customers both on AWS and not yet on AWS.
Training: Fully subsidized training and enablement programs are available.
Events: Depending on the use case, eligibility, and cost, we can either fully subsidize or co-invest in joint webinars, SKOs, and customer-facing events—provided there’s a defined solution focus, target audience, pipeline target, and measurable ROI.
On average, how much MDF credits can we anticipate receiving monthly/annually from the TDSynnex program? We would like to see details as this is important to us.
Will we be assigned a dedicated account manager, technical expert or help with pre-sales and design? (saving strategies and rightsizing recommendations are critical).
Yes, you’ll have a dedicated AWS account manager, plus access to technical SMEs and pre-sales engineering resources. Again, who would be part of our team, Gina?
Given the breadth of AWS service delivery designations and competencies (e.g., MSP, migrations, government, education, to name a few), our AWS practice provides technical expertise and support across migrations & modernization, managed services, professional services, assessments, and AWS workloads.
Additionally, our ServiceSolv team provides engineering, integration, deployment, and remediation services which enables you to offer end-to-end customer lifecycle support without building these functions internally. We can also provide white-labeled resources delivered under your branding, where applicable.
Net 90 terms
Extended payment terms are feasible through TD SYNNEX. We'd have to get you in contact with our credit/finance team to review your eligibility If 90 is denied for whatever reason, what is your standard, 60? 2% Net 10?

Hello Aptum team,

Nice to e-meet you all.

Thank you for your due diligence, you’re asking all the right questions. I did my best to respond succinctly in email, but I’d love to walk through each point in more depth on a call, especially if you’re interested in how we differentiate compared to other AWS distributors.
Custom cost-management tools and Security dashboards via StreamOne Ion?

Yes, the StreamOne platform provides pre-built dashboards and the ability to create custom reports, including configurable alerts. These reports can show usage, consumption trends, and other financial insights.

StreamOne also offers real-time reporting on IaaS consumption (Azure, AWS, and Google), enabling advanced cost-optimization capabilities and continuous billing visibility, not just month-end reporting.

In addition, StreamOne includes a SecOps Portal for partners, providing a centralized dashboard to view and manage customer security features and policies.

If you need more specific details, I’m happy to follow up.
Access to AWS funding programs, ACE deal registrations and leads?

Yes, As a Premier Tier AWS Partner, we help our partners leverage AWS mechanisms and programs, including offerings, funding, enablement, and guidance on ACE deal registrations as part of co-sell execution (where eligible and aligned to AWS criteria).

The TD SYNNEX AWS team also has a dedicated Customer Success and Conversion team focused on customer lead generation and demand generation.

We’ve built an infrastructure framework around programs like SBAI and other third-party lead-gen mechanisms to maintain, hydrate, nurture, and route end-customer leads to our partners—across both Commercial and Public Sector.
API access or automated insights for billing data independent of AWS (i.e., aligning payment terms, managing outstanding balances are important to us)
Yes, both are available. I can connect you with the team that managed this process so they can provide a detailed overview and answer any specific questions.
What GTM strategies do you offer? Free training and discounted workshops? Co-sponsored AWS events?

Yes, TD SYNNEX offers several GTM motions to help partners build and scale pipeline, including enablement, co-marketing, and co-sell support.

Pipeline: Top-of-funnel, assessment-led sales plays for customers both on AWS and not yet on AWS.
Training: Fully subsidized training and enablement programs are available.
Events: Depending on the use case, eligibility, and cost, we can either fully subsidize or co-invest in joint webinars, SKOs, and customer-facing events—provided there’s a defined solution focus, target audience, pipeline target, and measurable ROI.
Will we be assigned a dedicated account manager, technical expert or help with pre-sales and design? (saving strategies and rightsizing recommendations are critical).

Yes, you’ll have a dedicated AWS account manager, plus access to technical SMEs and pre-sales engineering resources.

Given the breadth of AWS service delivery designations and competencies (e.g., MSP, migrations, government, education, to name a few), our AWS practice provides technical expertise and support across migrations & modernization, managed services, professional services, assessments, and AWS workloads.

Additionally, our ServiceSolv team provides engineering, integration, deployment, and remediation services which enables you to offer end-to-end customer lifecycle support without building these functions internally. We can also provide white-labeled resources delivered under your branding, where applicable.
Net 90 terms
Extended payment terms are feasible through TD SYNNEX. We'd have to get you in contact with our credit/finance team to review your eligibility 
 
Thanks,
Michael

Hi Adam & Team,
 
I wanted to share the clarification via email as well to ensure the whole team was notified.
 

When a Partner moves from Disti A to Disti B, their discounts move with them. If a Program Account or Technology Engagement Program Account was Base eligible, it will continue to receive Base discount, regardless of spend, UNLESS EUR is changed to Internal. The same applies for any account receiving POD/PSD/CEI through the sunset period (up to March 31, 2027).

Meaning no Shareshift discount will apply here, and Aptum will receive the 8% front-end discount on all accounts/eligible AWS services regardless of spend. I've also attached a doc that outlines this as there are some exceptions, but it relates directly to Billing Transfer and NCI.

HI Marc,
 
Please find below the answers to your questions.
 
We have clients that are using AWS native tools for cost reporting and we are keen to understand when/what changes with their experience.
 
From your customer’s perspective, there should be no change to their experience when it comes to using AWS native cost reporting tools. Services such as Cost Explorer, CUR (Cost and Usage Reports), and other AWS-native billing and reporting capabilities will continue to function exactly as they do today.The transition to TD SYNNEX as the distribution partner primarily affects the billing and distribution relationship on the backend, not the operational environment within AWS.  
 
We are wondering if there is a two hop route to implementing this change and what the impact would be to our margin. Phase  1) just make TD our distribution , Phase 2) move some customers over to new model that removes Root for Distribution. 
 
Now, there isn’t a simple or automatic way to transfer accounts between different distribution models. While initiating the transition to TD SYNNEX as the distributor is certainly possible, moving accounts across structural models typically requires careful coordination and review on a case-by-case basis to ensure the transition is handled smoothly.
I would also recommend reviewing the exit clause with your current distributor. In most cases, partners are required to provide a 90-day notice period before making a move to another distributor. Ensuring this is aligned early in the process helps avoid any delays or contractual complications during the transition.
 
Kindly revert on any clarifications.

Hi Christine, Gina & Rupesh
Please treat as priority and address comments in blue:
 
Custom cost-management tools and Security dashboards via StreamOne Ion?
Yes, the StreamOne platform provides pre-built dashboards and the ability to create custom reports, including configurable alerts. These reports can show usage, consumption trends, and other financial insights.
StreamOne also offers real-time reporting on IaaS consumption (Azure, AWS, and Google), enabling advanced cost-optimization capabilities and continuous billing visibility, not just month-end reporting.
In addition, StreamOne includes a SecOps Portal for partners, providing a centralized dashboard to view and manage customer security features and policies.
If you need more specific details, I’m happy to follow up.
Please provide detailed steps how you will assist us with the change-over from another aggregator to TDS?
Access to AWS funding programs, ACE deal registrations and leads?
Yes, As a Premier Tier AWS Partner, we help our partners leverage AWS mechanisms and programs, including offerings, funding, enablement, and guidance on ACE deal registrations as part of co-sell execution (where eligible and aligned to AWS criteria).
The TD SYNNEX AWS team also has a dedicated Customer Success and Conversion team focused on customer lead generation and demand generation. Who exactly would be part of our team? Where does Gina fit into the picture and how can we utilize her as a resource?
We’ve built an infrastructure framework around programs like SBAI and other third-party lead-gen mechanisms to maintain, hydrate, nurture, and route end-customer leads to our partners—across both Commercial and Public Sector.
API access or automated insights for billing data independent of AWS (i.e., aligning payment terms, managing outstanding balances are important to us)
Yes, both are available. I can connect you with the team that managed this process so they can provide a detailed overview and answer any specific questions.
What GTM strategies do you offer? Free training and discounted workshops? Co-sponsored AWS events?
Yes, TD SYNNEX offers several GTM motions to help partners build and scale pipeline, including enablement, co-marketing, and co-sell support.
Pipeline: Top-of-funnel, assessment-led sales plays for customers both on AWS and not yet on AWS.
Training: Fully subsidized training and enablement programs are available.
Events: Depending on the use case, eligibility, and cost, we can either fully subsidize or co-invest in joint webinars, SKOs, and customer-facing events—provided there’s a defined solution focus, target audience, pipeline target, and measurable ROI.
On average, how much MDF credits can we anticipate receiving monthly/annually from the TDSynnex program? We would like to see details as this is important to us.
Will we be assigned a dedicated account manager, technical expert or help with pre-sales and design? (saving strategies and rightsizing recommendations are critical).
Yes, you’ll have a dedicated AWS account manager, plus access to technical SMEs and pre-sales engineering resources. Again, who would be part of our team, Gina?
Given the breadth of AWS service delivery designations and competencies (e.g., MSP, migrations, government, education, to name a few), our AWS practice provides technical expertise and support across migrations & modernization, managed services, professional services, assessments, and AWS workloads.
Additionally, our ServiceSolv team provides engineering, integration, deployment, and remediation services which enables you to offer end-to-end customer lifecycle support without building these functions internally. We can also provide white-labeled resources delivered under your branding, where applicable.
Net 90 terms
Extended payment terms are feasible through TD SYNNEX. We'd have to get you in contact with our credit/finance team to review your eligibility If 90 is denied for whatever reason, what is your standard, 60? 2% Net 10?
If Aptum has an AWS customer that goes bankrupt or denies us payment, what programs do you have to reduce this burden? Historically, we’ve had to pay for AWS consumption and cannot collect from a bankrupt customer…
Besides 10th of the following month billing; what other sweeteners can you add to incentivize Aptum to award TDSynnex over a competitor?

April 1st AWS Program Changes

Distributors move to back-end rebate only (no front-end discount)
New billing transfer program creates visibility silos between customer/partner/distributor
TDS Model:
100% pass-through: "You get what you earn"
No tiered programs—partners receive full earned discounts
Root exception & billing transfer capabilities ready now
Additional Opportunity: Fintech Customer Migration

Opportunity: TDS can build exception case based on historical migration work and APFP submissions
Billing transfer allows account capture while customer retains root access

Partner Growth Incentive lost for one year when switching distributors

Hello Aptum team,

Nice to e-meet you all.

Thank you for your due diligence, you’re asking all the right questions. I did my best to respond succinctly in email, but I’d love to walk through each point in more depth on a call, especially if you’re interested in how we differentiate compared to other AWS distributors.
Custom cost-management tools and Security dashboards via StreamOne Ion?

Yes, the StreamOne platform provides pre-built dashboards and the ability to create custom reports, including configurable alerts. These reports can show usage, consumption trends, and other financial insights.

StreamOne also offers real-time reporting on IaaS consumption (Azure, AWS, and Google), enabling advanced cost-optimization capabilities and continuous billing visibility, not just month-end reporting.

In addition, StreamOne includes a SecOps Portal for partners, providing a centralized dashboard to view and manage customer security features and policies.

If you need more specific details, I’m happy to follow up.
Access to AWS funding programs, ACE deal registrations and leads?

Yes, As a Premier Tier AWS Partner, we help our partners leverage AWS mechanisms and programs, including offerings, funding, enablement, and guidance on ACE deal registrations as part of co-sell execution (where eligible and aligned to AWS criteria).

The TD SYNNEX AWS team also has a dedicated Customer Success and Conversion team focused on customer lead generation and demand generation.

We’ve built an infrastructure framework around programs like SBAI and other third-party lead-gen mechanisms to maintain, hydrate, nurture, and route end-customer leads to our partners—across both Commercial and Public Sector.
API access or automated insights for billing data independent of AWS (i.e., aligning payment terms, managing outstanding balances are important to us)
Yes, both are available. I can connect you with the team that managed this process so they can provide a detailed overview and answer any specific questions.
What GTM strategies do you offer? Free training and discounted workshops? Co-sponsored AWS events?

Yes, TD SYNNEX offers several GTM motions to help partners build and scale pipeline, including enablement, co-marketing, and co-sell support.

Pipeline: Top-of-funnel, assessment-led sales plays for customers both on AWS and not yet on AWS.
Training: Fully subsidized training and enablement programs are available.
Events: Depending on the use case, eligibility, and cost, we can either fully subsidize or co-invest in joint webinars, SKOs, and customer-facing events—provided there’s a defined solution focus, target audience, pipeline target, and measurable ROI.
Will we be assigned a dedicated account manager, technical expert or help with pre-sales and design? (saving strategies and rightsizing recommendations are critical).

Yes, you’ll have a dedicated AWS account manager, plus access to technical SMEs and pre-sales engineering resources.

Given the breadth of AWS service delivery designations and competencies (e.g., MSP, migrations, government, education, to name a few), our AWS practice provides technical expertise and support across migrations & modernization, managed services, professional services, assessments, and AWS workloads.

Additionally, our ServiceSolv team provides engineering, integration, deployment, and remediation services which enables you to offer end-to-end customer lifecycle support without building these functions internally. We can also provide white-labeled resources delivered under your branding, where applicable.
Net 90 terms
Extended payment terms are feasible through TD SYNNEX. We'd have to get you in contact with our credit/finance team to review your eligibility 
 
Thanks,
Michael

To: 'Campeau, Linda' <Linda.Campeau@tdsynnex.com>
Subject: AWS Cloud Marketplace
 
Hi Linda,
I need your help steering me in the right direction: We resell AWS to a customer in the NA geography with an annual spend is $6M. We’d like to have a discussion with your AWS team on this opportunity, kindly pass me along.
 
Thanks,  
 

Matthew Carter
Senior Global Director, Supply Chain
+1 (416) 847-0852
aptum.com | LinkedIn

If you have received this message in error, please notify the sender immediately, destroy this email and any attachments, and do not use, copy, store and/or disclose to any person this email and any attachments.

items.subject: Can you direct me Re: Aptum's success
items.body_text:
[Jo Holloway]
You don't often get email from j.holloway@aiglobalmedialtd.com. Learn why this is important 

Good day Adam

I've been trying to reach out regarding your success in Corporate Vision Magazine's brand-new CV Technology Excellence Awards 2026 programme, but I'm concerned you might not be the right contact for this. Could you direct me?

Based on internal research, the team here at Corporate Vision have recognised Aptum in the upcoming awards as our: Best Cloud Computing Solutions Firm 2026 - Canada.

If I am in the right place, great! At this stage I'm just following up to make sure you are aware that you've been awarded, and find out how you would like to progress with this as we won't highlight you on our website without consent.

If I'm not, I would be grateful if you could provide the correct contact details so I can follow up directly on this.

I look forward to hearing from you.

Kind regards,

Jo Holloway
Brand Coordinator, Corporate Vision Magazine

T: +44 (0) 1283 365191
Book in a call with me: https://calendly.com/j-holloway/30min

Registered Company Number: 07400916 | ICO Registration: ZA269476. Registered Address: AI Global Media Ltd, Ground Floor, Suites B-D, The Maltsters, Wetmore Road, Burton on Trent, Staffs, DE14 1LS.

Any views or opinion expressed are solely those of the author and do not necessarily represent those of the company. Our Company maintains a strict zero-tolerance policy against any form of harassment. Subsequent action will be taken in line with our anti-harassment policy should our employees experience any form of harassment.

This email has been sent to adam.nelson@aptum.com. To opt-out from receiving future correspondence, please respond to this email with "UNSUBSCRIBE" and we will action this. Our opt-out process is automated and may take up to 2 working days to complete.

items.subject: Aptum // What we know
items.body_text:
[Mason Goodridge]
You don't often get email from mason@goodfithq.cc. Learn why this is important 

Hey Adam, 

We're in a tough spot… we supply data to GTM Teams but the problem is that people have been doing this for a while, and really badly.

We like to flex our muscles to show we're offering a depth of data/insight never previously possible. Honestly, people are usually surprised what we have access to.

Can I share with you some data on Aptum you don't even know yourself? 

Mason Goodridge
Growth Associate @GoodFit  

PS. I'll try and make the example something you'd like to know about prospects too.

͏ ­   ͏ ­   ͏ ­   ͏ ­   ͏ ­   ͏ ­   ͏ ­   ͏ ­   ͏ ­   ͏ ­   ͏ ­   ͏ ­   ͏ ­   ͏ ­   ͏ ­ 

 

 
Meet Claude Opus 4.7 

Opus 4.7 is a notable upgrade for advanced coding and complex professional work, with particular gains on the most difficult tasks. You can hand off long-running work with confidence. It pays precise attention to instructions, verifies its own outputs, and produces higher-quality interfaces, slides, and docs. 

If you're on a Premium seat, Opus 4.7 is now your default in chat, Cowork, and Claude Code. If you're on a Standard seat, your default stays Sonnet 4.6, but you can switch to Opus 4.7 for complex work. 

Try Opus 4.7 

Cowork is now generally available 

Cowork is out of research preview and available to all Team plan users. It's built for work that takes real effort, pulling from multiple sources, making sense of it all, and producing finished outputs. Research briefs, contract review, financial analysis, polished decks from scattered materials. Available on the Desktop app.

Give Claude a folder and it reads what's there, figures out what's relevant, and saves finished work back to the same place. You can run multiple tasks at once, each in its own conversation, and track progress from the sidebar.

Not sure when to use Chat vs. Cowork vs. Claude Code? Here's a quick way to think about it:
Chat is for quick questions, brainstorming, iterative drafting, and working through problems back and forth.
Cowork is for complex or sustained work—research, analysis, and producing finished documents and deliverables—where Claude needs room to pull from multiple sources and see things through.
Claude Code is for building software—writing, testing, running, and deploying code, with access to your file system and development tools.

See the full guide 

 
Works across your Microsoft Office apps 

Claude is now available as an add-in for Excel, PowerPoint, and Word. Each one puts Claude in a sidebar inside the app, but the real power is when you use them together.
Describe a task that spans multiple files and Claude handles the coordination. Pull numbers from an Excel model into a PowerPoint deck. Summarize a Word document into slides. Draft a memo using data already in a spreadsheet. You stay in one place while Claude does the switching.
Claude for Word is available in beta. It reads, drafts, and edits your documents while preserving formatting. The edits land as tracked changes so you stay in control of what gets accepted.
Note: To use these add-ins, your admin needs to enable them via the Microsoft Marketplace.

Learn how it works 

 
Go from idea to polished visual 

Claude Design is a new Anthropic Labs product in research preview. Describe a deck, a prototype, or a one-pager, and Claude builds a first draft you can refine through conversation. Export to PPTX, PDF, HTML, or Canva when you're done. 

Start designing 

 
Run multiple agents from a single window 

The Claude Code desktop app has been redesigned for parallel work. You can now run multiple agents, review their work, and manage everything from one place. The new layout includes an integrated terminal, in-app file editing, HTML/PDF previews, and a faster diff viewer — all drag-and-drop so you can arrange it how you want. 

Learn how it works 

Get even more out of Claude 

 
How to pick the right model for better results 

Here’s a 3-minute guide to matching the best model for the work you do. 

Read the guide 
 
Go deeper with Cowork 

Take this free course covering the task loop, plugins, file workflows, and how to steer multi-step work. 

Try Cowork 

Was this email useful?
 

 

 

  

Anthropic PBC, 548 Market St, PMB 90375, San Francisco, CA 94104 

This email was sent to adam.nelson@aptum.com. To opt out of future emails, click unsubscribe.
