items.subject: April resources to drive renewals and operational readiness
items.body_text:
[Microsoft AI Cloud Partner Program]
Plus: Explore the latest incentives, skilling, and program updates.

Having trouble viewing this email? | View as a web page

 

 

 

Final reminder: Extended service terms enforcement begins May 4
 
Effective May 4, 2026, we're aligning renewal and end-of-term options to create a more consistent and predictable experience.
 

Learn more 

 

Customers and partners will have clear choices at expiration: renew to a new term, cancel at end of term, or temporarily extend service through a paid extended service term (EST).
As a reminder:
Effective May 4, 2026, subscriptions with the following criteria will automatically transition to EST on expiry: 
Purchased or renewed on or after April 1, 2025
AND with an expiration date on or after May 4, 2026, and
Auto renew is set to off
If EST is not a desired end-of-term option, partners can select “cancel” or “renew” before the subscription’s end date.
Selecting “cancel” as an end-of-term option means the subscription ends on its expiration date, and the customer will lose access to the service.
Partners should prioritize renewal discussions with customers in advance of a subscription’s end date and discuss the best option for their business needs before the end of their service to avoid unexpected billing.
Learn more about EST, available resources, and how to export a list of EST subscriptions.
Update on multifactor authentication enforcement for Partner Center APIs
As previously communicated, multifactor authentication (MFA) enforcement for all Partner Center app+user APIs has started and will unfold in phases. API calls that do not support MFA may start to fail and return the following error:
HTTP Response Code: 401
Error Code: 900421
To avoid disruption, ensure your application integrations are updated to use MFA authentication for app+user scenarios.
Power Apps per app SKU returned to CSP price list in April 
As previously communicated, the Power Apps per app SKU was removed from the public price list effective January 2, 2026. This change was not intended to apply to the Cloud Solution Provider (CSP) channel; however, a temporary operational issue resulted in the SKU not appearing on CSP price lists in March 2026.
Microsoft has remedied this issue, and the Power Apps per app SKU is available again in the CSP price list. CSP partners and customers can continue to:
Purchase, use, and renew Power Apps per app subscriptions.
Add licenses for new customers and manage renewals, true-up, and true-down through standard CSP processes.
For additional details, please refer to the partner FAQ.
From pilots to production: How partners will drive Frontier Transformation 
AI has moved well beyond experimentation to production, and customers want AI that’s secure, governed, and scalable. Microsoft partners are turning that higher bar into opportunity—going beyond deployments to deliver durable outcomes with security at the center.
To support our ecosystem in driving this Frontier Transformation, Microsoft continues to invest in skilling, incentives, and go-to-market resources so you can build capability, scale repeatable offers, and deliver with confidence.
Read the State of the Partner Ecosystem to learn how AI is reshaping customer expectations, where partners are creating the most impact today, and how you can lead the next AI frontier.
Microsoft 365 pricing and packaging updates are coming in 2026—start renewal and upsell conversations now 
As previously announced, Microsoft is expanding the availability of security and management capabilities across commercial Microsoft 365 suites this year. Along with these added features, there will be a global pricing update across all purchasing channels effective July 1, 2026.
This is a key moment for you to drive renewals and upgrades ahead of the price update. Customers who renew before July 1 retain current pricing until their next renewal term, and you can unlock more revenue by attaching high‑value suites.
Lock in current pricing for Microsoft 365 Copilot Business bundles before July 1 or upsell to Microsoft 365 Business + Microsoft 365 Copilot Business bundles at renewal to support AI adoption for the small and medium enterprise customer segment.
Upsell to Microsoft 365 E3 or E5 for customers ready for advanced security, productivity, and IT management.
Activate incentives and targeted promotions from the Global Promo Readiness Guide to close deals before the pricing and packaging updates.
Successful renewal efforts can take up to three months, so now is the time to accelerate customer conversations and lock in price savings with one‑year or three‑year terms. Review the Partner Center announcement for more details and resources.
Four ways to build momentum and scale your security practice
Reduce early deal friction, create new attach opportunities, and build repeatable small and medium-sized business (SMB) motions.
Microsoft Sentinel 50-GB commitment tier promotion extended through June 30, 2026: Customers who purchase during this window lock in pricing through March 31, 2027—giving you a more predictable entry point for small and medium enterprise customers who want clarity on costs up front.
Save on Microsoft Purview Suite for Business Premium: Customers who have licensed Business Premium with Microsoft 365 Copilot Business or Microsoft 365 Copilot qualify for 50% off enhanced data protection on Purview Suite for Business Premium. This limited-time offer has been extended until July 1, 2026.
Expand customer reach with agents: Build and publish Microsoft Security Copilot agents in the Microsoft Security Store, making them discoverable within Security Copilot and Microsoft Security products.
Get SMB-ready talk tracks and tools in one place: Explore the Microsoft Security SMB partner hub for guidance, tools, and resources to lead stronger customer conversations and accelerate Microsoft Purview and Defender Suites for Business Premium scenarios.
For more details and updates, check out the Security partner newsletter.
Find and activate incentives in one place with the new digital incentives guide 
Now delivered through an online experience, the Microsoft Commercial Partner Incentives Guide gives you clear navigation, direct paths to engagement, and faster access to up-to-date guidance.
Search incentives by category to find opportunities that align with your go-to-market priorities.
Quickly confirm eligibility to make informed decisions about where to invest time and effort.
Act swiftly on opportunities with live links that connect you to the next action.
Explore the new digital incentives guide experience and share your feedback in our upcoming partner community calls (Americas/EMEA; Asia) as we continue to evolve the experience.
April partner investments updates: Eligibility changes and new resources
Explore the latest updates to plan your upcoming engagements with clearer eligibility and stronger alignment across solution areas.
Security engagements in Microsoft Commerce Incentives (MCI)
Education (EDU) customers are no longer eligible, keeping the program aligned to its intended commercial security scenarios and funding model.
Each customer organization is limited to two deliveries per engagement, reinforcing quality execution, balanced coverage, and broader partner participation.
AI Business Solutions engagements in MCI
Strategic 500 (S500) customers are no longer eligible, aligning to the Microsoft investment portfolio strategy.
Copilot + Power Accelerate remains eligible for S500 customers.
Co-op marketing guideline updates
We've updated the minimum co-op threshold associated with the Hosting incentive, as well as clarified sub-contracting language.
Azure Accelerate reminder
Partners with Azure specializations can earn Azure Accelerate investments when migrating eligible customers to Azure and when supporting software development companies as they migrate their customers. Review the terms and conditions in the incentives guide.
Localized Business Premium Immersion Briefing kits
Advanced Security for Business Premium Immersion Briefing kits are now available in French, Spanish (LATAM), Portuguese (Brazilian), German, Italian, and Japanese.
ISV Success Advanced: Build and Publish update 
Copilot Studio and Power Automate are now eligible workloads for the AI layer, expanding supported build scenarios. Review the terms and conditions in the incentives guide.
Build your AI fluency at a Sales Certification Week 
Our new three-day virtual Sales Certification Week is designed for partners to build AI fluency and turn AI conversations into real customer opportunities.
Participants will gain practical AI sales skills and prepare for the AB-730 (AI Fundamentals and Business Value) and AB-731 (AI Strategy, Adoption, and Governance) certifications. 
Register early—space is limited.
May 5–7 (EMEA - German) 
May 12–14 (Americas - Spanish) 
May 13–15 (Asia - Chinese,  Japanese, Korean) 
May 18–20 (EMEA - French) 
May 19–21 (Americas - Portuguese) 
More skilling resources to move your business forward 
Explore the new Partner Skilling Catalog to find on-demand training across Microsoft cloud solutions.
Move deals forward with Deal Ready Demo Accelerators—ready-to-demo scenarios built on Microsoft's latest technologies—and quickly demonstrate real customer outcomes without engineering effort or a live tenant.
Find the right distributor to scale your business
Partnering with a distributor is a requirement for CSP indirect resellers—and an opportunity to simplify operations while gaining a competitive edge.
By offloading billing, provisioning, compliance, and platform management to a distributor, your team can focus on closing deals and building customer relationships. Plus, a distributor equips you with the go-to-market campaigns, incentives, skilling resources, and repeatable, scalable motions to close renewals faster and expand revenue.
Explore our network of authorized distributors to find the right fit for your CSP business.
Microsoft 365 Fundamentals retired: Stay on track with Modern Work skilling 
To keep the Microsoft AI Cloud Partner Program aligned with evolving market demand, we have retired the Microsoft 365 Fundamentals certification, effective March 31, 2026.
Microsoft 365 Fundamentals certifications earned before March 31, 2026, will continue to count toward skilling points for the Solutions Partner for Modern Work designation through March 31, 2027. We will continue to share guidance and new skilling options—including planned alternatives to Microsoft 365 Fundamentals like the Copilot and Agent Administration Fundamentals certification—to support your skilling journey.
Review certification requirements now so you can continue to qualify for designations and demonstrate your knowledge and skills to customers with confidence. We will continue providing resources and guidance to support your transition.
 

 

 

 
 
 

 
 

 

Copyright 2026 Microsoft Corporation.
 

You have received this message from Microsoft as a result of your participation in the Microsoft AI Cloud Partner Program. This message contains important information on your general membership, or your benefits as a member.
 
Privacy Statement
 

Microsoft Corporation
One Microsoft Way
Redmond, WA 98052
